findings.description: The title block in the PDF is centered and appears in a larger/bolder font, while the HTML renders it as left-aligned smaller text without the same visual prominence.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale...' centered in larger font. HTML: Same text left-aligned in smaller, lighter styling.

findings.description: Section headings 'Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing' are underlined in the PDF but rendered as larger bold headings without underline in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: underlined headings in body text size. HTML: larger bold headings without underline, styled as H2-level headings.

findings.description: The PDF header block is centered with facility info, while the HTML uses a left-aligned header block separated by a horizontal rule.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2: centered header with facility name and address. HTML page 2: left-aligned header with horizontal divider line.

findings.description: The mailing address block for Mr. Adam Ulishney is indented in the PDF but left-aligned (with less indentation) in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: address block indented significantly from left margin. HTML: address block with less indentation.

findings.description: The URL in the PDF is presented as a hyperlink but is rendered as plain text in the HTML without an anchor tag.
findings.evidence: PDF contains 'https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx' as a clickable link. HTML renders it as plain text without an <a> tag.

findings.description: No h1 heading is present in the HTML. The document title information appears only in a page-header div. The two section headings are rendered as h2 elements without a parent h1.
findings.evidence: The HTML has no <h1> element. The document header 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL' is in a <div class='page-header'> rather than an <h1>. Two <h2> elements exist: 'Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing'.

findings.description: The page header block is duplicated for both pages, which mirrors the PDF's repeated headers. The outer <main> wrapping also contains nested <main> elements, which is structurally incorrect HTML but does not duplicate content beyond what appears in the PDF.
findings.evidence: Two instances of the page-header div with identical content appear, matching the PDF's two-page header repetition.
